name: Garlic Green Beans
description: Need a simple, go-to way to cook green beans without too much fuss? This garlic green beans recipe is it, and it goes with everything!
recipeIngredient: 3 tablespoons neutral oil ((such as vegetable oil, canola oil, or avocado oil))
recipeInstructions: Bring a pot of water to a boil. Blanch the green beans for 2 minutes. Drain. (No need to shock them in cold water.)

Heat a dry wok or large skillet over medium-high heat, and add the oil. When the oil is shimmering, add the green beans, and let them fry until they begin to scorch and wrinkle, about 2 minutes. Lower the heat to medium, and add the water, garlic, and salt to taste. Stir-fry for 1 minute, just until the garlic turns golden. Serve!

ai.cliffNotes: Blanch first, then flash-fry — tender beans with a blistered exterior.
keyTips: Pre-blanch the beans so the stir-fry just blisters the outside without long cooking.
Line them up and trim all the stem ends in one cut.
Skip the ice bath — drain and go straight to the hot pan for better blistering.
